chunks.text: The EECS Department sponsors a number of undergraduate research and support programs geared to underrepresented groups. Our two affiliated colleges also offer special graduate recruitment programs. The EECS department is committed to fair treatment, access, opportunity, and advancement for all students at every stage of their education and career development. The BPE website offers updated and focused information to encourage and spark the interest of a diverse variety of students.

The Computer Science Scholars Program offers a 'home' within Cal for students from underserved backgrounds who might not have had access to resources that prepare them to succeed in computer science. The SRC Intel Undergraduate Research Opportunity matches undergraduates with faculty mentors for sustained summer or academic year semiconductor research.

The Computer Science Kickstart Program is a one-week introductory class to computer science targeting incoming UC Berkeley students who are curious about computer science. The Summer Undergraduate Program in Engineering Research at Berkeley invites undergraduates to spend 9 weeks on campus over the summer conducting research on 'Collecting and Using Big Data for the Public Good' with supervision from EECS faculty and grad students.

The L&S Berkeley Science Network (BSN) is a College of Letters & Science program designed to increase the number of underrepresented students who acquire advanced degrees in STEM fields. The COE Engineering Preview Days program guides junior year students selected for this College of Engineering program to put together a competitive Ph.D. application to Berkeley.

Engineering programs for diversity, equity and inclusion support first-generation students, underrepresented minorities, women, transfer students, veterans and others, which help our students develop academically, professionally and as leaders. Campus-wide programs for diversity, equity, and inclusion are provided by the UC Berkeley Division of Equity and Inclusion to support all campus community members.
